page.content: In Table 1, the data reveals various cellular measurements categorized by two conditions, Control 72 (n = 6) and LPS 72 (n = 7). For the row labeled Cell Numbers (106), the values are 28.4 ± 3.9 for Type II Cells and 6.2 ± 2.2 for Plate Cells under Control, while LPS shows 33.2 ± 5.6 for Type II Cells and 7.8 ± 2.2 for Plate Cells. The % Type II Cells row indicates 75 ± 2 for Control and 83 ± 1 for LPS, while the Plate Cells are at 22 ± 3 and 19 ± 3, respectively. The % Macrophages shows 11 ± 2 and 12 ± 2 for Control Type II and LPS Type II, respectively, with Plate Cells at 56 ± 5 and 59 ± 4. In the % Neutrophils row, Control has no value, while LPS reports 2 ± 1 for Type II Cells and 22 ± 3 for Plate Cells. The % Other Cells row lists 14 ± 1 for Control Type II, while LPS has 3 ± 3* for Type II and no value for Plate Cells. Table 2 outlines a sequence of phrases, starting with Step #1: Functional OR physical, continuing to Step #2: Capacity OR performance, followed by Step #3: Evaluation OR assessment, culminating in Step #4: #1 AND #2 AND #3, and concluding with Step #5: #4 AND reliability.
